Let $f_n(x)$ be defined recursively by
$$ f_0(x) = x, \quad f_1(x) = f(x), \quad f_{n+1}(x) = f(f_n(x)), \quad \text{for } n \ge 0, $$
where $f(x) = 1 + \sin(x - 1)$.

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item Show that there is a unique point $x_0$ such that $f_2(x_0) = x_0$.
\item Find $\displaystyle{\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} \frac{f_n(x_0)}{3^n}}$ with the above $x_0$.
\end{enumerate}
